items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.
items.author: Saad Naja

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.
items.author: Michael S. Selig

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Crypto's worst two-day liquidation in months deepens as investors chase the AI trade elsewhere
items.subheadline: BTC price crashed to $61,300 before recovering to $62,500 as $3 billion in liquidations hit over two days and traders loaded up on $60,000 puts anticipating further losses.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: JPMorgan sees shrinking window for U.S. crypto market structure overhaul
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act still faces several legislative hurdles, and disputes over stablecoin yield have emerged as a key sticking point, the bank said.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Standard Chartered's three 'Ifs' that stand between bitcoin and a market low: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: Your day-ahead look for June 4, 2026
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Russia sanctions British teenager for alleging A7A5 use in funding Ukraine war
items.subheadline: The A7A5 stablecoin was designed to bypass sanctions imposed on Russia following its invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Moomoo adds Kalshi prediction markets, giving users access to event contracts
items.subheadline: Retail trading platform moomoo has partnered with Kalshi to offer CFTC-regulated event contracts tied to economic data, elections and major sporting events directly within its app.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: How the House Financial Services Committee is taking on tokenization: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: Rep. French Hill, head of the House Financial Services Committee, weighed in on outstanding policy issues in an interview with CoinDesk last month.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: The time is now: the Senate must act on crypto market structure legislation
items.subheadline: The United States needs to finally establish the clear framework that the market needs.
items.author: Summer Mersinger

items.headline: Bitcoin’s biggest quantum risk may not be wallet keys. An early investor fears something bigger
items.subheadline: Andrew Gault, the venture capitalist who funded the quantum hardware labs now threatening bitcoin, says the industry is looking in the wrong place. Google's own security team moved in the same direction in March.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Polymarket says No for May, Yes for June after Strategy's recent bitcoin sale
items.subheadline: UMA voters ruled that Strategy's June 1 disclosure counted for the June contract, even though the company said it sold bitcoin during the final week of May.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: Franklin Templeton is teaming up with MoonPay to let big investors swap stablecoins for yields 24/7
items.subheadline: The Wall Street giant is integrating its tech platform with MoonPay's infrastructure, allowing eligible institutions to seamlessly swap stablecoins for yield-generating tokenized funds without ever leaving the blockchain.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) faces an 'identity crisis' and DeFi devs need to stop acting like tech bros
items.subheadline: Solstice Labs CEO Ben Nadareski says developers must act like financial managers to win back institutional trust amid ongoing security exploits.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Coinbase (COIN) backs Ethena (ENA) ahead of savings product launch for 100 million users
items.subheadline: Coinbase Ventures, the exchange's venture arm, bought Ethena tokens as the protocol is set to roll out a Coinbase integration next week.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Big tech is 'terrified' of AI agents wiping out ad revenue, says Billions Network CEO
items.subheadline: Evin McMullen’s view on AI agents disrupting Google’s and Facebook’s business model was previously shared by Cardano Founder Charles Hoskinson and Cloudflare CSO Stephanie Cohen.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Franklin Templeton says Wall Street fears blockchain because it threatens its profits
items.subheadline: Jenny Johnson, Franklin Templeton's CEO, said blockchain and crypto threaten a huge number of business models that exist today in traditional finance.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: TradFi will sit out DeFi growth until security issues are resolved, executives say
items.subheadline: Lenders are particularly interested in blockchain's back-office applications, but security failures are blocking wider adoption.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Coinbase said to be looking into participating new stablecoin platform backed by Stripe, Visa, Mastercard
items.subheadline: U.S. crypto exchange Coinbase is also said to be looking into the possibility of participating in the new stablecoin platform.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Kraken parent plans to offer tokenized IPO access as investors await SpaceX, Anthropic debut
items.subheadline: Payward aims to give retail investors access to IPO shares at the offering price through tokenized equities.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Every single bank will soon need to hold digital assets, says Zodia Custody CEO Julian Sawyer
items.subheadline: Sawyer confirmed that Standard Chartered’s full acquisition of the firm is on track to target a signing at the end of June and complete by the end of August.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Tom Lee's Bitmine (BMNR) to offer preferred stock with 9.5% dividend, following Strategy's playbook
items.subheadline: The largest Ethereum treasury firm is taking a page from Michael Saylor's Strategy to issue preferred shares to tap new sources of funding.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Goldman Sachs teams with Apex, Archax for tokenized real estate fund
items.subheadline: The fund combines blockchain native issuance with established fund structures.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: ADA under 20 cents as Hoskinson says he is 'taking a break' after warning of ecosystem failures
items.subheadline: Hoskinson's remarks followed a string of setbacks for the ecosystem, including the cancellation of Cardano's flagship conference and the shutdown of a prominent analytics platform.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: Bitcoin supply in loss overtakes profit, a hallmark of bear-market bottoms
items.subheadline: More than half of the bitcoin in circulation is sitting on unrealized losses as the BTC price tests historically significant bear-market support levels.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: XRP Ledger's design blocks the flash loan attacks costing DeFi hundreds of millions
items.subheadline: A draft XRPL amendment notes that flash loan attacks are "structurally impossible" on the network because of how its transactions are built, an architectural quirk that has spared the chain from the exploit class that has cost Ethereum DeFi billions.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Three Sui mainnet halts in 48 hours traced to an upgrade bug by developers
items.subheadline: The Sui Foundation's post-mortem published Sunday traces all three outages to interactions between a new address-balance feature shipped in the v1.72 release and the network's existing gas and consensus logic.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Whitehat developer unlocks $2 million stuck in a 2016 Ethereum ICO contract for nine years
items.subheadline: 0xflorent, a security researcher, found an integer-overflow flaw in the HongCoin token sale contract that lets the team unlock funds for 48 original investors. It is the second such recovery he has publicized in eight days.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum's Vitalik Buterin is rethinking how DeFi handles market crashes
items.subheadline: In a research post published Monday, Buterin proposed creating index-tracking assets using options contracts rather than the debt-based structures that underpin much of DeFi today.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Movement pivots to stablecoin payments as the layer-2 boom loses momentum
items.subheadline: The team behind Movement said it plans to leverage licensed payment partners alongside blockchain settlement rails to target the roughly $685 billion remittance market serving low and middle-income countries.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Titan Network hits 4 million devices on its crowdsourced AI-compute network
items.subheadline: Titan Network built its crowdsourced computing infrastructure on a slim $1 million seed round, signing up tech giants like Tencent and Alibaba.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Bitcoin’s compute power dwarfs top 100 supercomputers by 600k times, says Bittensor co-founder
items.subheadline: Ala Shaabana explains how using Bitcoin network’s coordinate-and-reward playbook can bust open corporate monopolies on AI.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Trezor says users' crypto is safe after Ledger researchers uncover chip flaw
items.subheadline: The flaw affects only one of the wallet's multiple security layers and would require physical access, specialized equipment and advanced expertise to exploit.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Apparent Zcash outage was a block explorer problem, infrastructure provider says
items.subheadline: One expert said the issue was mainly with block explorers tracking the onchain activity.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: How DeFi is changing the financial landscape for Latin Americans
items.subheadline: DeFi is quietly shifting from niche crypto experiment to a legitimate financial tool across the region, explains Serrano.
items.author: Sebastián Serrano

items.headline: It’s time for clarity for America’s digital asset markets
items.subheadline: Recent polls have shown that registered voters want America to set the rules for global finance, reinforcing why the Senate must act now, explains Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Iran war shows markets no longer sleep
items.subheadline: The latest conflict involving Iran has produced an unexpected proving ground for financial infrastructure, and an unlikely winner has emerged, argues Huang.
items.author: Annabelle Huang

items.headline: The U.S. can’t lose the bitcoin race to China
items.subheadline: The next global power competition is not being fought over missiles alone. It’s being fought over money, and right now, China is moving aggressively to shape the future of it, argues Gooden.
items.author: Congressman Lance Gooden

items.headline: The agentic CFO in your pocket
items.subheadline: Chalom explains that retail investors have never had the opportunity to access and manage their own digital treasury desk. Until now.
items.author: Joseph Chalom

items.headline: The Clarity Act won’t lead to adoption without crypto tax reform
items.subheadline: The legislation is being framed as a turning point for U.S. crypto policy, but there’s a major piece of the puzzle being overlooked, argues Singh.
items.author: Robin Singh

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.
items.author: Ji Hun Kim

items.headline: Japan's ruling party supports crypto ETF trading, yen-based stablecoins
items.subheadline: Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party said the country should create a legal framework for trading crypto ETFs in a proposal to the finance minister.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: U.S. sanctions Nobitex, other Iranian crypto exchanges amid ongoing war
items.subheadline: OFAC said it was blacklisting Nobitex and three other Iranian exchanges, as well as a few executives, citing links to terror and other crimes.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: UK House of Lords committee calls on Bank of England to reconsider proposed stablecoin restrictions
items.subheadline: The Bank of England proposed limits of 20,000 pounds per coin for individuals and 10 million pounds for businesses.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Banks' survey says people don't want to rock the boat if stablecoin yield risks lending
items.subheadline: The American Bankers Association, which lobbies against the crypto sector over the Clarity Act's stablecoin section, unveiled its new polling.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Premier League soccer clubs warned about unauthorized crypto firms' sponsorship
items.subheadline: The FCA said unauthorized firms could be breaching rules on financial promotions through high-profile sponsorships deals.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Crypto PACs go undefeated in June primaries as Fairshake scores bipartisan winning streak
items.subheadline: Winners included Democrats Zoe Lofgren, Ted Lieu, Dave Min and Rob Menendez, as well as Republican Sen. Mike Rounds, with Democrats accounting for 10 of the 11 victories.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: New DeFi entrant widens field of crypto political campaign funds as elections loom
items.subheadline: The Defend Developers PAC is aiming to contribute to lawmakers who seek to shield crypto developers from legal vulnerabilities.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin bounces, HYPE falls, NEAR gets demolished as crypto deals with a wipe out
items.author: Stephen Alpher

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) isn't broken, says Strategy's (MSTR) Saylor
items.subheadline: Bitcoin's drop reflects capital rotation into artificial intelligence, Saylor argues.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Bitcoin Cash (BCH), up 1.5%, is only gainer
items.subheadline: NEAR Protocol (NEAR) declined 15.2% and Internet Computer (ICP) dropped 13.1%, leading the index lower.
items.author: CoinDesk Indices

items.headline: XRP price news: What next for Ripple-linked token as it sinks to four-month lows
items.subheadline: XRP fell another 7% after losing key support levels, with traders weighing growing institutional demand against one of the token's weakest technical setups in months.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Tom Lee's $250,000 ether (ETH) target would imply $2 million per bitcoin (BTC)
items.subheadline: Bitmine's chairman told a Paris conference ether would 50x from here on AI and corporate validators. Here's how supply schedule, the ETH-to-bitcoin ratio history and the actual breakdown of staked ether look at those levels.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Not all layer 2s are dying, but many no longer have a reason to exist
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at the state of the Ethereum layer-2 ecosystem.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Crypto due diligence has changed: three questions advisors should revisit
items.subheadline: As stablecoins, shifting regulation and AI-enabled infrastructure mature, advisors should revisit three questions their crypto due diligence may no longer fully cover.
items.author: Beth Haddock

items.headline: Hyperliquid's HYPE drops 10% as Arthur Hayes exits position despite $150 price target
items.subheadline: The crypto veteran blamed macro risks and AI mania for taking profits, drawing backlash from traders for selling well below his recent bullish forecasts.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Nouriel Roubini's business partner sees bitcoin crashing 70% before rallying to $500,000
items.subheadline: Backed by economist Nouriel Roubini, a long-time anti-bitcoin advocate, and known as 'Dr. Doom,' the Atlas CEO, Reza Bundy, shot a short-term warning for bitcoin but stayed bullish in the long-term.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: OCC chief says Democrats applying sole political pressure in World Liberty charter choice
items.subheadline: The regulator rejected claims he's doing President Trump's bidding during a congressional hearing that also addressed the GENIUS Act stablecoin push.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto Clarity Act in spotlight for bad-actor provisions as Senate process grinds forward
items.subheadline: The industry has been trying to make a case this week the Clarity Act provides law enforcement strong tools to combat illicit finance involving cryptocurrency.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Zcash plummets 30% as Shielded Labs reveals a major bug that went undetected for four years
items.subheadline: Shielded Labs revealed that the bug could have helped an attacker print unlimited counterfeit tokens. That could have damaged trust in the token's supply and its value.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Bitcoin plunges to near $62,000 as the AI trade unwinds, HYPE falls 14%
items.subheadline: Broadcom's disappointing AI chip outlook pulled the Nasdaq lower for a third session, dragged Asian equities down with it, and took crypto along for the ride.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: BTC price updates: Bitcoin could fall to $60,000, Zcash plunges 37%
items.subheadline: AI bets are recording rapid profit-taking as Broadcom as outlook for AI-chip sales fell short of high expectations, driving down crypto markets.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
